subject: Product Management
title: Juicero
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Juicero.

hook.text: Juicero was a $400 WiFi-connected juicer that squeezed proprietary fruit bags. Then people tried squeezing them by hand.

buildup.text: The device had 400 custom parts, produced four tons of pressing force, and required a WiFi connection to verify bag freshness.

discovery.text: Juicero raised $120 million from top investors. The pitch: be the 'Keurig of juice' — a subscription for cold-pressed juice.

twist.text: Bloomberg reporters discovered you could squeeze the bags by hand and get the same juice. The $400 machine was unnecessary.

climax.text: The story went viral. Juicero became a symbol of Silicon Valley excess — overengineering a problem that didn't exist.

punchline.text: If users can solve the problem with their hands, you don't need a machine.

quiz.question: What made Juicero a product failure?
options: The expensive machine was unnecessary — users could squeeze the bags by hand
The subscription was too cheap to sustain the business
